examples.title: FCFS Scheduling Algorithm
examples.description: First Come First Serve scheduling implementation showing process selection from ready queue
examples.code:
ALGORITHM FCFS_Schedule(processList)
BEGIN
  readyQueue = CreateQueue()
  FOR each process IN processList DO
    Enqueue(readyQueue, process)
  END FOR

  currentTime = 0
  totalWaitingTime = 0

  WHILE NOT IsEmpty(readyQueue) DO
    currentProcess = Dequeue(readyQueue)

    waitingTime = currentTime - currentProcess.arrivalTime
    totalWaitingTime = totalWaitingTime + waitingTime

    currentTime = currentTime + currentProcess.burstTime

    currentProcess.completionTime = currentTime
  END WHILE

  avgWaitingTime = totalWaitingTime / NumberOfProcesses
  RETURN avgWaitingTime
END
examples.explanation: This FCFS algorithm processes jobs in order of their arrival time. It maintains a ready queue, processes each job to completion, calculates waiting time as current time minus arrival time, and computes average waiting time.

examples.title: Round Robin Scheduling Simulation
examples.description: Implements Round Robin CPU scheduling with time quantum and calculates waiting times
examples.code:
def round_robin_scheduler(processes, time_quantum):
    """
    Processes: list of (arrival, burst) tuples
    Returns: average waiting time
    """
    n = len(processes)
    remaining_burst = [p[1] for p in processes]
    arrival = [p[0] for p in processes]
    waiting_time = [0] * n

    current_time = 0
    completed = 0
    queue = []
    index = 0

    # Add processes arriving at time 0
    for i in range(n):
        if arrival[i] == 0:
            queue.append(i)

    while completed < n:
        if not queue:
            # Find next arriving process
            current_time = min(arrival[i] for i in range(n) if remaining_burst[i] > 0)
            for i in range(n):
                if arrival[i] == current_time:
                    queue.append(i)
            continue

        process_idx = queue.pop(0)

        # Execute for time quantum or remaining burst
        if remaining_burst[process_idx] <= time_quantum:
            current_time += remaining_burst[process_idx]
            remaining_burst[process_idx] = 0
            completed += 1
            waiting_time[process_idx] = current_time - arrival[process_idx] - (processes[process_idx][1] - remaining_burst[process_idx])
        else:
            current_time += time_quantum
            remaining_burst[process_idx] -= time_quantum

        # Add newly arrived processes to queue
        for i in range(n):
            if arrival[i] <= current_time and i not in queue and remaining_burst[i] > 0:
                queue.append(i)

        # Add current process back if not completed
        if remaining_burst[process_idx] > 0:
            queue.append(process_idx)

    avg_waiting = sum(waiting_time) / n
    return avg_waiting

# Example usage
processes = [(0, 10), (0, 5), (0, 8)]
time_quantum = 4
print(f"Average waiting time: {round_robin_scheduler(processes, time_quantum)}")
examples.explanation: This Python implementation simulates Round Robin scheduling. Each process gets a time quantum (4 units), and if not complete, goes back to the ready queue. It handles arrival times and calculates waiting times for each process.

examples.title: SJF Non-Preemptive Scheduling
examples.description: Shortest Job First algorithm that selects process with minimum burst time
examples.code:
ALGORITHM SJF_NonPreemptive(processList)
BEGIN
  readyQueue = CreateQueue()
  completedList = CreateList()
  currentTime = 0
  totalWaitingTime = 0

  WHILE completedCount < TotalProcesses DO
    // Add all arrived processes to ready queue
    FOR each process IN processList DO
      IF process.arrivalTime <= currentTime AND NOT process.completed THEN
        AddToReadyQueue(process)
      END IF
    END FOR

    // Find process with minimum burst time
    shortestJob = FindMinBurst(readyQueue)

    // Calculate waiting time
    waitingTime = currentTime - shortestJob.arrivalTime
    totalWaitingTime = totalWaitingTime + waitingTime

    // Execute the process
    currentTime = currentTime + shortestJob.burstTime
    shortestJob.completionTime = currentTime
    MarkAsCompleted(shortestJob)

    RemoveFromReadyQueue(shortestJob)
  END WHILE

  avgWaitingTime = totalWaitingTime / TotalProcesses
  RETURN avgWaitingTime
END

FUNCTION FindMinBurst(queue)
BEGIN
  minProcess = queue[0]
  FOR each process IN queue DO
    IF process.burstTime < minProcess.burstTime THEN
      minProcess = process
    END IF
  END FOR
  RETURN minProcess
END
examples.explanation: SJF (non-preemptive) selects the process with the shortest burst time from the ready queue at each scheduling decision point. It provides optimal average waiting time when all processes are available at time 0.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n²) for simple implementation, O(n log n) with priority queue

examples.title: Priority Scheduling with Aging
examples.description: C program implementing priority scheduling with aging to prevent starvation
examples.explanation: This C program implements priority scheduling with aging to prevent starvation. Aging increases the effective priority of waiting processes over time, ensuring that low-priority processes eventually get CPU time even when higher-priority processes keep arriving.
